symptom: MetLinkR failed to link together metabolites in 18 of 31 unique manual linkage cases due to an inability to parse and/or map identifiers, representing a hard floor on recall imposed by identifier quality.

The tool groups 20 metabolite pairs that a manual curator considered distinct (unique MetLinkR linkages), indicating that RefMet name collisions can cause false positive cross-dataset linkages, particularly for non-isomeric compounds with shared names.

Datasets providing only a single identifier type (common name only, e.g., file9, file11, file13) have no second identifier column against which conflicts can be detected, limiting the ambiguity-flagging capability.
Mapping quality depends on the version of the locally cached RaMP-DB SQLite database; users should verify the version with RaMP::listAvailableRaMPDbVersions() to ensure currency.
